Pricing follows a freemium, dual cloud/self-host model: the Free plan costs nothing forever and includes unlimited workspaces, collections, requests and test runners with community support on Hoppscotch's hosted cloud service; the Organization plan costs 6 dollars per user per month billed annually (with a higher monthly rate available) and adds an admin dashboard, dedicated support and custom payment options; separately, the Self-Host Community Edition is free and open source under the MIT license for organizations that want to run Hoppscotch entirely on their own servers; and a Self-Host Enterprise Edition costs 19 dollars per user per month billed monthly and adds SAML-based single sign-on, OpenID Connect, advanced admin tools, audit logs and domain whitelisting for larger, security-conscious organizations. Hoppscotch's target audience spans individual developers and open-source contributors who want a fast, free, no-signup-required API client, through to engineering teams and enterprises that need self-hosted, compliant API testing infrastructure with SSO and audit logging. Its most direct competitors are Postman, the dominant commercial API platform it was explicitly built as an alternative to, along with Insomnia, Thunder Client, and Bruno, another open-source, offline-first API client; Hoppscotch differentiates through being fully open source with a genuinely usable self-hosted option, a browser-first Progressive Web App experience that requires no installation, and broad protocol support beyond plain REST, including GraphQL and realtime protocols like WebSocket and MQTT that some competitors handle less natively.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Hoppscotch began in 2019 as Postwoman, a browser-based API request builder created by Kerala-based developer Liyas Thomas. It was rebranded to Hoppscotch in 2020 following a community naming contest, and has since grown into a widely used open-source alternative to commercial tools like Postman, surpassing roughly 80,000 stars on GitHub.

The company behind the project, Hoppscotch Inc, is headquartered in Kochi, India, and raised a 3 million dollar seed round in late 2021 led by OSS Capital with participation from Automattic, the maker of WordPress. It remains a small, largely remote team that leans heavily on its open-source community for contributions and feature development.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Hoppscotch supports building and testing requests across REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, Server-Sent Events, Socket.IO and MQTT, all from a fast, browser-based Progressive Web App that requires no installation, alongside a downloadable desktop app and a CLI for running collections in CI/CD pipelines.

Collections, folders and environment variables keep requests organized across projects and deployment targets, while pre-request and post-request scripting enables automated assertions and dynamic values. Team workspaces add role-based collaboration, and a built-in proxy resolves CORS restrictions when testing APIs directly from the browser.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Hoppscotch's cloud Free plan costs nothing and includes unlimited workspaces, collections, requests and test runners, making the core product free forever for individuals and small teams. The Organization plan adds an admin dashboard and dedicated support for 6 dollars per user per month billed annually.

For organizations that must keep data on their own infrastructure, the Self-Host Community Edition is free and MIT-licensed, while a Self-Host Enterprise Edition costs 19 dollars per user per month and adds SAML SSO, OpenID Connect, audit logs and domain whitelisting for larger, security-conscious teams.

features.title: Multi-protocol request builder
features.description: Builds and sends requests across REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, Server-Sent Events, Socket.IO and MQTT.

features.title: Progressive Web App
features.description: Runs directly in the browser with offline support and no installation required.

features.title: Desktop and CLI clients
features.description: Offers a native desktop app plus a command-line interface for running collections in CI/CD pipelines.

features.title: Pre/post-request scripting
features.description: Automates assertions, dynamic variables and test logic before and after each request.

features.title: Team workspaces
features.description: Role-based collaboration lets teams share collections and environments securely.

features.title: Self-hostable backend
features.description: MIT-licensed self-host Community Edition lets organizations run Hoppscotch entirely on their own infrastructure.

features.title: Built-in CORS proxy
features.description: Resolves cross-origin restrictions when testing APIs that would otherwise be blocked in-browser.

features.title: Customizable interface
features.description: Themeable UI with multiple color schemes and layout options.

pros: Fully free core product with no signup required to start testing APIs
Genuinely open source with a real, MIT-licensed self-hosted option
Broad protocol support including GraphQL, WebSocket and MQTT, not just REST
Fast, lightweight browser-based Progressive Web App
Large, active open-source community driving continuous improvement
cons: Smaller ecosystem of third-party integrations compared to Postman
Enterprise-grade SSO and audit logging require the paid Self-Host Enterprise tier
Team collaboration and admin features are less mature than established commercial rivals
Small company size (roughly 8-20 people) behind a widely used product
Documentation and mock server tooling are less extensive than Postman's

faqs.question: Is Hoppscotch free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hoppscotch's core cloud product is free forever, including unlimited workspaces, collections, requests and test runners, and there is also a free, MIT-licensed self-hosted edition.

faqs.question: Who created Hoppscotch?
faqs.answer: Hoppscotch was created by Liyas Thomas in 2019 under the name Postwoman and rebranded to Hoppscotch in 2020. The company, Hoppscotch Inc, is headquartered in Kochi, India.

faqs.question: Can Hoppscotch be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Self-Host Community Edition is free and open source under the MIT license, and a paid Self-Host Enterprise Edition adds SSO, audit logs and domain whitelisting for 19 dollars per user per month.

faqs.question: How much does the Hoppscotch Organization plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Organization plan costs 6 dollars per user per month billed annually and adds an admin dashboard, dedicated support and custom payment options on top of the free tier.

faqs.question: What protocols does Hoppscotch support?
faqs.answer: Hoppscotch supports REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, Server-Sent Events, Socket.IO and MQTT, in addition to standard HTTP methods.

faqs.question: What are the main alternatives to Hoppscotch?
faqs.answer: Common alternatives include Postman, Insomnia, Thunder Client and Bruno, another open-source, offline-first API client.

use_cases.title: Quick API testing without installation
use_cases.description: Developers open Hoppscotch in the browser to send and inspect API requests instantly without creating an account.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted API testing for compliance
use_cases.description: Security-conscious organizations self-host Hoppscotch to keep all API request and response data on their own infrastructure.

use_cases.title: CI/CD API test automation
use_cases.description: Teams use the Hoppscotch CLI to run collections and assertions automatically as part of continuous integration pipelines.

features: Advanced admin tools and audit logs
